sourcingNotes.poolAssessment: Merricks was expected to be the hardest place to source — the Tier A plan flagged it as a likely deferral. Instead, Wikimedia Commons holds an extraordinary find: a CC-BY 4.0 working vineyard image explicitly tagged to Merricks, Victoria, at 5303x3047 resolution. This is the single best clean-licence find in the entire sourcing pass. CC-BY 4.0 has no share-alike contamination — the SA policy question does not apply.
sourcingNotes.majorFinding: Vineyard Merricks Grove Victoria Australia.jpg — CC-BY 4.0 by BrooksieG. The Tier A plan §8 listed place-merricks-01 as a probable deferral. This image cancels the deferral.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY 4.0. Clean licence — attribution required, no share-alike. Zero contamination risk. This is the strongest licence in the entire Tier A pool after CC0 candidates.
candidates.place: Merricks, Victoria — working vineyard, early Spring
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED PRIMARY. This image passes ALL ten §6 rules — the first candidate in the entire Tier A pipeline to do so without any override needed. CC-BY 4.0 licence is clean. Image description explicitly says 'Working in vineyard in Merricks, Victoria in early Spring' and links to the Mornington Peninsula wine Wikipedia article. Resolution (5303x3047) gives excellent crop headroom for 16:9 hero. Taken October 2023 — recent. The brief specifically warned this slot would likely defer to Phase 2; this candidate cancels the deferral entirely. Human figure may be present (working in vineyard) but per §6 rule 6, humans as scale/activity are permitted — only 'model as primary subject' is rejected.

editorActionsRequested.question: Only one candidate available — the pool for Merricks-specific imagery on Commons is exactly one file. However, this single candidate passes all ten §6 rules and has a clean CC-BY 4.0 licence. Recommend approving despite the thin shortlist. If the editor rejects, the slot defers to Phase 2 as originally expected.
